channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Covalency modulation doping enables durable high-voltage operation in NiO-based all-solid-state electrochromic devices
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 25 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71949-0

NiO electrochromic devices degrade under high voltage due to strengthened metal–oxygen bonding and metastable phase accumulation. Here, authors tune covalency via Mo doping to enable regenerable heterojunctions, achieving over 17,000 cycles with high optical contrast and efficiency.
item.dc:title: Covalency modulation doping enables durable high-voltage operation in NiO-based all-solid-state electrochromic devices
item.dc:creator: Dukang Yan
Huawei Bai
Liwei Cao
Yang Li
Shuokun Sun
Sunan Tian
Yuwei Zhao
Xiang Zhang
Ang Li
Xiaoxu Liu
Mingjun Chen
Jiupeng Zhao
Yao Li
Xiaodong Han

item.title: Hierarchical heterogeneous ordering enables wide-temperature strength-ductility synergy in additively manufactured medium-entropy alloys
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72261-7

An additively manufactured medium entropy alloy with hierarchical ordering maintains high strength and ductility from 77 to 873 K, offering a pathway to structural materials capable of reliable performance across wide temperature ranges.
item.dc:title: Hierarchical heterogeneous ordering enables wide-temperature strength-ductility synergy in additively manufactured medium-entropy alloys
item.dc:creator: Yun Cheng
Bo Li
Fu-Zhen Xuan
